International NGOs operating in Afghanistan include WFP (World Food Programme), FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization), and ICRC (International Committee of the Red Cross). Local organizations like the Afghanistan Research and Evaluation Unit (AREU) continue limited research operations. UNAMA (United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan) monitors human rights and governance.

Afghanistan's formal corporate sector is largely non-functional under current conditions. International engagement occurs through multilateral development organizations and humanitarian agencies. The Afghanistan Investment Authority was established to attract foreign investment but is currently non-operational.

FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) — maintains agricultural programs in Afghanistan focused on food security and sustainable farming. UNODC (United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime) — publishes annual Afghanistan Opium Survey tracking cultivation area, yield, and farm-gate prices. World Bank Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) — the largest single source of development funding for Afghanistan, currently operating in limited capacity under yaptırımlar constraints.

Note: Afghanistan under Taliban rule has no elected legislature or representative democratic institutions. International engagement channels include: UN Security Council yaptırımlar regime (Resolution 2255), which allows humanitarian exemptions. The Doha process for political dialogue. Afghan diaspora organizations in Europe, North America, and Australia. International development partners coordinate through the Geneva Conference on Afghanistan.

AL.s: AB adayı country with small open ekonomi ($27B GSYİH). Oil production (1.04% rents) centered in Patos-Marinzë field. Services-dominant (48.7% GSYİH) with significant hydropower potential (95%+ of electricity from hydro).

sectors.w: Albania's oil sector is small and concentrated in aging onshore fields. As EU accession negotiations require çevresel acquis alignment, the örtüşen çıkar between oil producers and government revenue zayıflar — EU çevresel standards make marginal fields unprofitable.
bp: EU accession çevresel alignment eliminates subsidies for marginal oil production. Ne zaman Albania must meet EU çevresel standards, örtüşen çıkar continued extraction from aging fields (many dating to communist era) disappears — cleanup costs exceed production revenue.

Uygulamak EU-aligned çevresel monitoring and emisyonlar disclosure. Albania's EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) membership provides a framework — extending to emisyonlar makes W visible.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel contamination at oil production sites to AKM (National Environment Agency)
Document health impacts on communities near oil fields — report to Institute of Public Health
Use Albania's ihbarcı protection law (Law 60/2016) for reporting çevresel violations
Albpetrol: Geliştirmek decommissioning and site remediation plan for aging oil fields
Yatırım yapmak yenilenebilir enerji diversification — Albania has significant untapped solar and wind potential
Benimsemek EITI enhanced reporting standards including çevresel metrics
AKM: Uygulatmak çevresel remediation requirements at legacy oil production sites
AKBN: Require çevresel bonds for all new exploration licenses
ERE (Energy Regulatory Entity): Accelerate yenilenebilir enerji permitting
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Kuvendi (Albanian Parliament) talep etmek için çevresel remediation of legacy oil sites
Support accelerated EU accession negotiations contingent on çevresel acquis compliance
Advocate for inclusion of çevresel cleanup costs in Albania's EITI reporting
Şunu talep edin: Energy Community Treaty enforcement on yenilenebilir enerji targets
i: AKM (Agjencia Kombëtare e Mjedisit / National Environment Agency) — responsible for çevresel monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. AKM conducts çevresel impact assessments for energy projects. AKBN (Agjencia Kombëtare e Burimeve Natyrore / National Agency of Natural Resources) — regulates oil and gas exploration and production licenses. Law 60/2016 'On Whistleblowing and Whistleblower Protection' — Albania's ihbarcı protection statute, adopted as part of EU accession reforms, providing protection against retaliation for reporting violations of law.

Albpetrol — Albania's devlete ait oil company, managing legacy fields from the communist era. Albpetrol's operations include some of Europe's most contaminated industrial sites. Bankers Petroleum (now Geo-Jade Petroleum, Chinese-owned) — the largest oil producer in Albania, operating the Patos-Marinzë field. EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — Albania has been an EITI member since 2013, publishing annual reports on oil and madencilik revenues.

ERE (Enti Rregullator i Energjisë / Energy Regulatory Entity) — Albania's independent enerji düzenleyicisi, responsible for licensing, tariff-setting, and market regulation in the electricity and natural gas sectors. Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy — oversees national energy policy including the National Energy Strategy 2030, which targets significant yenilenebilir enerji expansion.

Kuvendi i Shqipërisë (Parliament of Albania) — unicameral legislature with 140 members elected by proportional representation. Citizens can contact their deputies through parlament.al. The Komisioni për Veprimtarinë Prodhuese (Committee on Production Activities) handles energy and çevresel legislation. Energy Community Treaty — Albania is a member of the Energy Community, which extends EU energy and çevresel acquis to Southeast European countries. Non-compliance can result in dispute settlement proceedings.

AM.s: Small landlocked ekonomi ($26B GSYİH) with significant madencilik sector (min=6.79% of GSYİH, primarily copper-molybdenum at Kajaran and Sotk gold mine). Services-dominant (62% GSYİH). Geopolitical constraints from Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and closed borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.

sectors.w: Armenia's madencilik sector generates significant water pollution (Debed and Voghji rivers) and tailings dam risks. Ne zaman downstream water quality data is published alongside mine production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir from bilateral transactions.
bp: Mandate çevresel bonds and rehabilitation deposits that fully internalize mine closure costs. Ne zaman madencilik companies must pre-fund çevresel remediation, örtüşen çıkar extraction without cleanup zayıflar — marginal mines become unprofitable.

Mandatory real-time water quality monitoring downstream of all madencilik operations, published openly. Armenia's EITI membership provides a disclosure framework — extending to çevresel monitoring data makes W visible from production data.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Open-pit madencilik inherently disturbs landscapes and generates acid mine drainage. However, modern tailings management and water treatment can reduce the magnitude of sistem bağımlılığı.
a: Report çevresel violations at mine sites to the Environmental and Mining Inspection Body
Document water pollution downstream of tailings facilities — report to the Ministry of Environment
Use Armenia's ihbarcı protection provisions under the Anti-Corruption Strategy for reporting
Uygulamak international best practice tailings management (Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management)
Benimsemek EITI enhanced reporting including çevresel impact metrics
Commission independent water quality assessments for all operations
Ministry of Environment: Güçlendirmek çevresel impact assessment requirements for new madencilik licenses
EMIB: Increase inspection frequency and enforce penalties for water pollution violations
Ministry of Territorial Administration: Require community consent processes for new mine approvals
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly) talep etmek için mandatory çevresel bonds for all madencilik operations
Support strengthening of EITI reporting requirements to include çevresel impact data
Advocate for EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) enforcement on çevresel standards
Şunu talep edin: international arbitration standards that include çevresel remediation in madencilik investment disputes
i: Environmental and Mining Inspection Body (EMIB) — the Armenian government agency responsible for monitoring compliance with çevresel regulations at madencilik operations, conducting inspections, and imposing yaptırımlar. Ministry of Environment — oversees çevresel policy, issues çevresel impact assessment approvals, and sets water quality standards. Armenia's Anti-Corruption Council — established under the Prime Minister's office, provides channels for reporting corruption and misconduct in the madencilik sector.

Zangezur Copper-Molybdenum Combine (ZCMC) — Armenia's largest madencilik company, operating the Kajaran copper-molybdenum mine. Lydian International / Lydian Armenia — operator of the controversial Amulsar gold mine project. GeoProMining — Russian-owned company operating gold mines in Armenia. EITI — Armenia became an EITI candidate country in 2017, requiring disclosure of madencilik revenues and contracts.

Ministry of Territorial Administration and Infrastructure — oversees madencilik policy and licensing. Competition Protection Commission — monitors market concentration in the madencilik sector. Armenian EITI Multi-Stakeholder Group — includes government, industry, and sivil toplum representatives overseeing extractive sector şeffaflık.

Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly of Armenia) — unicameral parliament with 107 members. Citizens can contact their representatives through parliament.am. Armenia's 2018 revolution established stronger democratic institutions. EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) — signed in 2017, includes provisions on çevresel protection and sustainable development that Armenia has committed to implement.

AO.s: Major African oil producer with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents ($29.6B). Sonangol (state oil company) dominates the ekonomi. Agriculture (22.1% GSYİH) is largely subsistence. Post-civil war recovery continues with high inequality.

sectors.w: Angola's oil production is declining from mature deepwater fields. As extraction costs rise and global demand plateaus, the örtüşen çıkar between Sonangol and international partners (TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil) zayıflar — declining reserves make diversification economically rational.
bp: Redirect Sonangol investment from deepwater exploration to yenilenebilir enerji and agricultural modernization. Angola's solar potential is enormous (2,500+ kWh/m²/year). Ne zaman renewable returns exceed marginal barrel returns from declining fields, the örtüşen çıkar breaks.

Uygulamak EITI-standard revenue şeffaflık extended to per-block çevresel impact data. Angola rejoined EITI in 2020 — extending disclosure to emisyonlar and çevresel costs makes W computable per production unit.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations at oil facilities to MIREMPET (Ministry of Mineral Resources, Petroleum and Gas)
Document community health impacts near production facilities through local NGOs
Report financial irregularities in Sonangol operations — Angola's anti-corruption framework (Lei 3/20) provides limited protections
Sonangol: Geliştirmek strategic diversification plan with binding yenilenebilir enerji investment targets
International partners (TotalEnergies, BP): Uygulamak Paris-aligned emisyonlar reduction plans for Angolan operations
Benimsemek TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all production sharing agreements
ANPG: Require çevresel impact assessments with community consultation for all new production blocks
Ministry of Environment: Güçlendirmek enforcement of çevresel regulations in the petroleum sector
Banco Nacional de Angola: Integrate climate risk into financial supervision of oil-dependent lending
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Assembleia Nacional talep etmek için publication of per-block çevresel impact data for all oil operations
Support EITI enhanced reporting with çevresel metrics for Angola
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: the African Union for continental yenilenebilir enerji investment targets
Şunu talep edin: Paris Agreement compliance monitoring through UNFCCC mechanisms
i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for çevresel policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced şeffaflık standards.

AR.s: G20 ekonomi ($638.4B GSYİH) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag GSYİH share but outsized çevresel footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the örtüşen çıkar — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to yenilenebilir enerji. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. Ne zaman renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the örtüşen çıkar between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well emisyonlar disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to emisyonlar makes W computable from production statistics.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report çevresel violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial çevre otoritesi (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo
YPF: Geliştirmek Paris-aligned transition plan with binding emisyonlar reduction targets for Vaca Muerta operations
Uygulamak TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
Yatırım yapmak CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities
Secretaría de Energía: Publish per-well emisyonlar data for all Vaca Muerta operations
SAyDS: Güçlendirmek federal çevresel standards for unconventional hydrocarbon extraction
ENARGAS: Reform yapmak natural gas tariff structure to eliminate implicit fossil fuel subsidies
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Diputado Nacional or Senador talep etmek için a timeline for fossil fuel subsidy phase-out and transparent per-well emisyonlar reporting
Güçlendirmek Argentina's NDC — current targets are considered insufficient by Climate Action Tracker
Leverage Mercosur-EU trade agreement çevresel provisions to align energy transition commitments
Support South American yenilenebilir enerji integration through regional grid interconnection
i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's çevre otoritesi, responsible for çevresel permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial çevresel coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal ihbarcı protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% devlete ait), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for çevresel policy and iklim değişikliği. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting emisyonlar of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU ekonomi ($534.8B GSYİH) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of GSYİH). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% GSYİH) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. Ne zaman per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: Uygulamak front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. Ne zaman consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the örtüşen çıkar between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products zayıflar.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. Reform yapmakulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate sistem bağımlılığı.
a: Report misleading health claims on food products to the Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (Federal Competition Authority)
Document undisclosed processing methods — report to AGES (Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety)
Use the HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) for protected reporting
Reform yapmakulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing — invest in minimally processed alternatives
Voluntarily adopt front-of-pack nutritional labelling ahead of EU mandate
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio using national health insurance data
BMSGPK: Uygulamak mandatory front-of-pack health labelling for UPF products
AGES: Publish annual diet-attributable disease burden data linked to specific product categories
Hauptverband der Sozialversicherungsträger: Release anonymized diet-related healthcare cost data
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Nationalrat or Bundesrat talep etmek için mandatory health-impact labelling on ultra-processed foods
Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting
Şunu talep edin: WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions through EU legislative process
i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent ekonomi with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited sivil toplum space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the örtüşen çıkar between SOCAR and international partners (BP) zayıflar — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance yenilenebilir enerji and non-oil economic development. Ne zaman SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the örtüşen çıkar breaks.
Uygulamak saha başına emisyonlar disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes emisyonlar data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document emisyonlar underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report çevresel concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: Geliştirmek binding yenilenebilir enerji transition plan with interim emisyonlar targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG emisyonlar şeffaflık standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: Benimsemek ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: Güçlendirmek çevresel standards for offshore Caspian operations
EITI Azerbaijan: Expand reporting to include çevresel impact data per production block
Tariff Council: Reform yapmak energy pricing to remove implicit fossil fuel consumption subsidies
Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: the Tehran Convention for Caspian Sea çevresel protection standards
Support EITI enhanced reporting with çevresel metrics
Şunu talep edin: European Energy Charter Treaty enforcement on çevresel standards in transit countries
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's çevre otoritesi, responsible for çevresel monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees çevresel impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive ihbarcı protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent şeffaflık mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs çevresel standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and çevresel issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict ekonomi ($29.6B GSYİH) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% GSYİH). AB adayı country. Significant coal madencilik and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the örtüşen çıkar between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. Ne zaman Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The örtüşen çıkar between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.

Mandatory emisyonlar monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned çevresel reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Kırılamaz. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report çevresel violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level çevresel inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): Geliştirmek coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
Yatırım yapmak yenilenebilir enerji capacity to replace aging coal plants
Uygulamak emisyonlar monitoring per facility ahead of EU ETS alignment
DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): Reform yapmak tariffs to reflect true çevresel costs of coal generation
Entity çevresel agencies: Uygulatmak air quality standards at coal power plants
Energy Community Secretariat: Use dispute resolution to enforce çevresel compliance
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Parlamentarna skupština talep etmek için coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community obligations
Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
Şunu talep edin: Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate çevresel agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — çevresel inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive ihbarcı protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in madencilik communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and çevresel acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large ekonomi ($450.1B GSYİH) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of GSYİH). High agricultural employment (11.2% GSYİH). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: Reform yapmak agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). Ne zaman subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, örtüşen çıkar monoculture between farmers and input suppliers zayıflar.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce sistem bağımlılığı.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension)
Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)
Report food safety violations in agricultural supply chains to BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority)
Agricultural input companies: Geliştirmek integrated pest management products as alternatives to chemical-intensive packages
Yatırım yapmak climate-resilient crop varieties through partnerships with BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute)
Benimsemek water-use efficiency targets across supply chains
Ministry of Agriculture: Reform yapmak subsidy structure from input-based to outcome-based
BWDB: Uygulamak mandatory groundwater monitoring networks in intensive agricultural zones
DoE (Department of Environment): Uygulatmak regulations on agricultural chemical runoff into waterways
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Jatiya Sangsad talep etmek için reform of agricultural subsidies toward climate resilience and crop diversification
Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance
Advocate for inclusion of groundwater depletion costs in national GSYİH accounting
Şunu talep edin: international climate finance to support agricultural diversification away from rice monoculture
i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive ihbarcı protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural Geliştirmekment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water Geliştirmekment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's çevresel regulatory agency, responsible for çevresel clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters ekonomi ($671.4B GSYİH). Services-dominant (71% GSYİH). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% GSYİH). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Uygulamak front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information zayıflar örtüşen çıkar high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. Reform yapmakulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate sistem bağımlılığı.
a: Report misleading health claims to SPF Économie (Federal Public Service Economy)
Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)
Use the Belgian ihbarcı protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Reform yapmakulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing
Extend voluntary Nutri-Score adoption to all product lines
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio
SPF Santé publique: Make Nutri-Score mandatory for all packaged food products
AFSCA/FAVV: Güçlendirmek enforcement of marketing claims on UPF products
INAMI/RIZIV: Publish annual reports linking food consumption patterns to healthcare expenditure
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers talep etmek için mandatory health-impact labelling
Support EU-wide mandatory Nutri-Score through the Farm to Fork Strategy
Advocate for WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
Şunu talep edin: inclusion of diet-related health costs in national accounting frameworks
i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian ekonomi ($23.1B GSYİH) with dominant gold madencilik sector (min=15.45% GSYİH). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% GSYİH). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold madencilik generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal madencilik), and community displacement. Ne zaman çevresel and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: Mandate çevresel bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial madencilik licenses. Ne zaman madencilik companies must pre-fund çevresel remediation and community compensation, örtüşen çıkar extraction without accountability zayıflar.

Publish maden başına çevresel impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to çevresel data makes W visible.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Open-pit gold madencilik inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce sistem bağımlılığı.
a: Report çevresel violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near madencilik operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal madencilik activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): Uygulamak Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
Benimsemek EITI enhanced reporting including çevresel impact metrics
Yatırım yapmak mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal madencilik support programs
Ministry of Mines: Güçlendirmek çevresel conditions in madencilik permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal madencilik regulation to reduce mercury use
EITI-BF: Expand reporting to include çevresel and community impact data
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: ECOWAS for regional madencilik çevresel standards
Support African Mining Vision implementation for sustainable resource governance
Şunu talep edin: international madencilik companies to adopt binding çevresel standards through OECD Due Diligence Guidance
Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national çevresel assessment authority, responsible for çevresel impact assessments of madencilik projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees çevresel policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and sivil toplum space is restricted.

Major madencilik companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international madencilik standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for madencilik policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and madencilik technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on madencilik revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes çevresel obligations for madencilik companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable madencilik governance.

BG.s: AB üyesi ekonomi ($113.3B GSYİH) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% GSYİH). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the örtüşen çıkar between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. Ne zaman Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The örtüşen çıkar breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory santral başına emisyonlar and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Kırılamaz. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report emisyonlar violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)
Use Bulgaria's ihbarcı protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Maritsa East complex operators: Geliştirmek coal phase-out plans with worker transition programs
Yatırım yapmak yenilenebilir enerji capacity (Bulgaria has strong solar potential — 1,500+ kWh/m²/year)
Benimsemek per-unit emisyonlar reporting aligned with EU taxonomy
KEVR (Energy and Water Regulatory Commission): Reform yapmak electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
Ministry of Energy: Geliştirmek binding coal phase-out timeline aligned with EU Green Deal
Ministry of Environment: Uygulatmak Industrial Emissions Directive compliance at all coal plants
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Narodno Sabranie (National Assembly) talep etmek için a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition funding
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out
Şunu talep edin: Territorial Just Transition Plans that include concrete retraining and economic diversification measures
i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional çevresel agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national çevresel monitoring agency collecting emisyonlar and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the devlete ait lignite madencilik company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent enerji düzenleyicisi, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B GSYİH) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the örtüşen çıkar breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC ekonomi. Ne zaman financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, örtüşen çıkar extraction breaks.
Publish saha başına çevresel and emisyonlar data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle emisyonlar visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document emisyonlar or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues
BAPCO: Accelerate the BAPCO Modernization Program to include yenilenebilir enerji components
Mumtalakat (sovereign wealth fund): Increase yenilenebilir enerji allocation in investment portfolio
Uygulamak TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
NOGA: Require emisyonlar disclosure for all petroleum operations
SCE: Güçlendirmek çevresel standards for refinery operations
CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain): Integrate climate risk disclosure into financial sector regulation
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: GCC Secretariat for regional carbon pricing mechanisms
Support IRENA recommendations on GCC energy transition pathways
Şunu talep edin: enhanced climate disclosure standards through the Bahrain Bourse ESG framework
Leverage Bahrain's UNFCCC commitments to strengthen national emisyonlar reduction targets
i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's çevre otoritesi, responsible for çevresel policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees çevresel impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the devlete ait oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited ihbarcı protection; sivil toplum space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic Geliştirmekment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a anayasal monarşi with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African ekonomi ($21.5B GSYİH) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% GSYİH, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% GSYİH). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. Ne zaman soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir from agricultural output data.
bp: Reform yapmak cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. Ne zaman input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the örtüşen çıkar between cotton traders and input suppliers zayıflar.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making çevresel and health costs visible makes W computable.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce sistem bağımlılığı — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)
AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton): Promote integrated pest management to reduce chemical dependency
Yatırım yapmak organic cotton certification and premium markets
Benimsemek soil health monitoring across cotton supply chains
MAEP (Ministry of Agriculture): Reform yapmak cotton input subsidy programs to incentivize sustainable practices
ABE: Güçlendirmek çevresel impact monitoring in cotton-growing zones
INSAE (National Statistics Institute): Include soil health and agricultural çevresel indicators in national surveys
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Député in the Assemblée Nationale talep etmek için reform of cotton input subsidies toward sustainable agriculture
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: ECOWAS for regional agricultural sustainability standards
Şunu talep edin: Cotton-4 (Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali) coordination on sustainable cotton production
Support WTO negotiations on cotton subsidy reform to level the playing field for African producers
i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's çevresel agency, responsible for çevresel impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of sivil toplum organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts çevresel monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B GSYİH) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the ekonomi. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the örtüşen çıkar between BSP and the government in continued extraction zayıflar — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. Ne zaman non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, örtüşen çıkar extraction breaks.
Uygulamak saha başına emisyonlar disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations at BSP facilities through Shell's global ethics and compliance channels
Document emisyonlar data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
Use BSP's internal reporting mechanisms for safety and çevresel concerns
BSP/Shell: Extend Shell's global net-zero commitments to Brunei operations
BIA (Brunei Investment Agency): Diversify sovereign wealth into yenilenebilir enerji and technology
Uygulamak TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all Brunei operations
PetroleumBRUNEI: Require emisyonlar monitoring and disclosure for all production licenses
Department of Environment: Güçlendirmek çevresel impact assessment requirements
AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam): Integrate climate risk into financial supervision
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: ASEAN for regional yenilenebilir enerji integration and emisyonlar reduction targets
Support UNFCCC mechanisms for small petrostate transition planning
Şunu talep edin: APEC energy working group initiatives on fossil fuel subsidy reform
Leverage Brunei's ASEAN chairmanship rotations to advance regional climate commitments
i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of Geliştirmekment, responsible for çevresel policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no ihbarcı protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on iklim değişikliği provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent ekonomi ($54.9B GSYİH) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and madencilik (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). Tarım 8.8% GSYİH with soy expansion driving ormansızlaşma. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. Ne zaman water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. Ne zaman indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, örtüşen çıkar extraction without consent zayıflar.
Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making çevresel costs visible makes W computable from production data.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.
a: Report çevresel violations at madencilik operations to the Autoridad de Fiscalización y Control Social de Bosques y Tierra (ABT)
Document water contamination or indigenous rights violations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Report safety violations at madencilik sites to the Ministry of Labor
YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos): Benimsemek DLE technology to minimize water consumption
Uygulamak community benefit sharing agreements with affected indigenous communities
Benimsemek international sustainability standards (IRMA — Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance)
Ministry of Mining: Güçlendirmek çevresel requirements for lithium extraction permits
MMAyA (Ministry of Environment and Water): Uygulatmak water extraction limits in Salar de Uyuni basin
AJAM: Require çevresel bonds for all madencilik concessions
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Diputado or Senador in the Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional talep etmek için mandatory prior consultation with indigenous communities for all lithium projects
Support ILO Convention 169 enforcement on indigenous consultation rights in extractive industries
Advocate for international standards on sustainable lithium extraction through the Lithium Triangle coordination (Bolivia, Argentina, Chile)
Şunu talep edin: inclusion of water depletion costs in madencilik revenue accounting
i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the madencilik administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing madencilik concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and çevresel rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state madencilik corporation overseeing Bolivia's madencilik sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's madencilik ministry, responsible for madencilik policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing çevresel policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian demokrasi. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island ekonomi ($15.8B GSYİH) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of GSYİH).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. Ne zaman beneficial ownership information and tax şeffaflık data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: Uygulamak full beneficial ownership şeffaflık in line with FATF recommendations. Ne zaman the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through şeffaflık, the örtüşen çıkar between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients zayıflar.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.
a: Report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) of The Bahamas
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to the Securities Commission of The Bahamas
Report regulatory violations to the Central Bank of The Bahamas
Benimsemek enhanced due diligence procedures for all client relationships
Uygulamak beneficial ownership şeffaflık standards ahead of regulatory mandates
Integrate climate risk assessment into financial products and client advisory services
Central Bank: Güçlendirmek beneficial ownership disclosure requirements for all licensed financial institutions
Securities Commission: Uygulatmak FATF recommendations on şeffaflık and anti-money laundering
Compliance Commission: Increase supervision of designated non-financial businesses and professions
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Member of Parliament in the House of Assembly talep etmek için enhanced financial şeffaflık legislation
Support OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Inclusive Framework participation
Leverage the Alliance of Small Island States (AOSIS) to link climate finance to financial şeffaflık commitments
Advocate for Caribbean regional coordination on beneficial ownership şeffaflık
i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas Finansal Hizmetler Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style parlamenter demokrasi. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income ekonomi ($19.4B GSYİH) with diamond madencilik historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% GSYİH). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the örtüşen çıkar between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium zayıflar — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. Ne zaman non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, örtüşen çıkar continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.

Publish full lifecycle çevresel data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all madencilik operations. Making çevresel costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Open-pit diamond madencilik inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term sistem bağımlılığı.
a: Report çevresel violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)
Document community displacement or inadequate compensation — report to the Ombudsman
Report safety violations to the Department of Mines
Debswana: Geliştirmek post-diamond economic transition plan with yenilenebilir enerji investment
De Beers: Extend lifecycle çevresel reporting to all Botswana operations
Benimsemek Kimberley Process Certification Scheme enhanced standards including çevresel metrics
Ministry of Minerals and Energy: Require çevresel bonds for all madencilik licenses
BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service): Ensure mineral royalties reflect true çevresel costs
Okavango Diamond Company: Lead on sustainability certification for Botswana diamonds
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Member of Parliament in the National Assembly talep etmek için a comprehensive post-diamond economic diversification strategy
Support African Union Mining Vision implementation for sustainable diamond governance
Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include çevresel and social standards
Şunu talep edin: SADC (Southern African Geliştirmekment Community) coordination on madencilik sustainability
i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for çevresel policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for madencilik safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees madencilik policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the parlamenter website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including madencilik.

BY.s: State-controlled ekonomi ($76B GSYİH) with significant manufacturing (20.3% GSYİH). Potash madencilik (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash madencilik generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. Ne zaman downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: International yaptırımlar (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the örtüşen çıkar by restricting Belaruskali's market access. Ne zaman sanctioned export revenues fall below çevresel remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based çevresel monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making çevresel damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Potash madencilik inherently generates salt waste. However, solution madencilik and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document çevresel violations through secure channels to international çevresel organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)
International potash buyers: Demand çevresel certification as condition of purchase (post-yaptırımlar)
Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian madencilik
Require independent çevresel audits as part of off-take agreements
International yaptırımlar bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export yaptırımlar until çevresel and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue çevresel performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)
IndustriALL: Monitor labor rights conditions at Belaruskali
Support maintenance of international yaptırımlar on Belaruskali until çevresel and governance reforms are implemented
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: UNECE for transboundary çevresel monitoring of potash madencilik impacts
Support Belarusian democratic opposition's platform for çevresel and governance reform
Şunu talep edin: international çevresel damage assessment of Soligorsk madencilik district
i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, sivil toplum, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective ihbarcı protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international çevresel organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's devlete ait potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK yaptırımlar since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under otoriter governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU yaptırımlar framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including çevresel governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished ekonomi ($71B GSYİH). Mining dominates (28.81% of GSYİH) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of GSYİH.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. Ne zaman supply chain traceability makes mine-level çevresel and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Ne zaman artisanal miners must meet labor and çevresel standards to access global markets, örtüşen çıkar unregulated extraction zayıflar.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking maden başına çevresel and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal madencilik with proper safety and çevresel management can reduce sistem bağımlılığı compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report çevresel or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): Uygulamak full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
Benimsemek IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
Yatırım yapmak formalization programs for artisanal madencilik communities
Ministry of Mines: Uygulatmak 2018 Mining Code çevresel provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: Güçlendirmek mineral certification to include çevresel and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal madencilik registration and oversight
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement talep etmek için enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code çevresel provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: the African Union for continental responsible madencilik standards
Şunu talep edin: OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale madencilik. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping madencilik sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines.
