Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees madencilik policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for madencilik sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent ekonomi ($15.7B GSYİH) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the örtüşen çıkar between international operators and government revenue zayıflar — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Ne zaman non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, örtüşen çıkar continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Uygulamak EITI-standard per-block revenue and emisyonlar disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to çevresel data makes W computable per production unit.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Uygulamak Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Geliştirmek economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Benimsemek per-block emisyonlar reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Güçlendirmek çevresel conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include çevresel impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: CEMAC for regional çevresel standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with çevresel metrics
Şunu talep edin: Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest ekonomi in francophone West Africa ($87.1B GSYİH). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of GSYİH with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require ormansızlaşma-free supply chains — when per-farm ormansızlaşma data is linked to cocoa transactions, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Ne zaman cocoa buyers must verify zero-ormansızlaşma, the örtüşen çıkar between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm çevresel data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal ormansızlaşma for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Uygulamak full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Yatırım yapmak cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Benimsemek living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Uygulatmak traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to çevresel compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Güçlendirmek enforcement against ormansızlaşma in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Député in the Assemblée nationale talep etmek için enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding ormansızlaşma targets
Şunu talep edin: living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end ormansızlaşma in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and çevresel regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to ormansızlaşma after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income ekonomi ($330.3B GSYİH) with massive madencilik sector (16.23% of GSYİH). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium madencilik depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Ne zaman maden başına water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir from madencilik output data.
bp: Uygulamak water pricing that reflects true scarcity in madencilik regions. Ne zaman miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, örtüşen çıkar water-intensive extraction methods zayıflar — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory maden başına water balance, tailings chemistry, and emisyonlar disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects çevresel data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report çevresel violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's ihbarcı law (Ley 21.015) and çevresel complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Benimsemek Science Based Targets for emisyonlar reduction across madencilik operations
Uygulamak ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase çevresel monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at madencilik operations
Sernageomin: Güçlendirmek tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Uygulamak the National Lithium Strategy with binding çevresel standards
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional talep etmek için mandatory water-neutral madencilik operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on çevresel access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for madencilik supply chains
Şunu talep edin: international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's çevresel enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with çevresel permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts çevresel impact assessments for madencilik projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's devlete ait copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper madencilik companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and madencilik service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees madencilik policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African ekonomi ($53.3B GSYİH) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% GSYİH — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the örtüşen çıkar between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue zayıflar — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Ne zaman REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, örtüşen çıkar continued extraction breaks.
Uygulamak EITI-standard per-block çevresel disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to çevresel data makes W computable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Geliştirmekment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Uygulamak çevresel monitoring and emisyonlar reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Geliştirmek post-oil diversification strategy
Benimsemek TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Güçlendirmek çevresel conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Uygulatmak çevresel impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to çevresel data
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Député in the Assemblée nationale talep etmek için transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: CEMAC for regional çevresel standards
Şunu talep edin: EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees madencilik and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American ekonomi ($418.8B GSYİH) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, örtüşen çıkar fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in yenilenebilir enerji. Ne zaman energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, örtüşen çıkar continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory saha başına emisyonlar disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to emisyonlar makes W computable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report çevresel violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
Uygulamak TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
Yatırım yapmak yenilenebilir enerji in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: Güçlendirmek çevresel licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: Reform yapmak energy tariffs to accelerate yenilenebilir enerji deployment
ANH: Uygulamak saha başına emisyonlar monitoring
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Representante or Senador in the Congreso to support the energy transition framework legislation
Güçlendirmek Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral emisyonlar reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance çevresel governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's çevresel licensing authority, responsible for çevresel impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% devlete ait), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the enerji düzenleyicisiy commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional çevresel authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income ekonomi ($95.4B GSYİH) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% GSYİH). Leader in ecotourism and çevresel policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Ne zaman per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: Reform yapmak agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Ne zaman export tax benefits require çevresel certification, the örtüşen çıkar between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production zayıflar.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and çevresel complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): Benimsemek pesticide reduction targets
Yatırım yapmak organic certification and agroforestry transitions
Uygulamak watershed-level çevresel monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): Reform yapmak export incentives to require çevresel certification
MINAE: Güçlendirmek pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative çevresel impact assessments for monoculture expansion
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa talep etmek için pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's çevresel reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Şunu talep edin: green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts çevresel impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for çevresel services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global çevresel leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on çevresel access rights.

CU.s: Command ekonomi ($107.4B GSYİH, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% GSYİH, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Ne zaman organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, örtüşen çıkar conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that sistem bağımlılığı on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document çevresel degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: Güçlendirmek çevresel monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
Şu yolla savunuculuk yapın: CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
Şunu talep edin: relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a tek parti devleti. Independent sivil toplum, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees çevresel policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island ekonomi ($37.6B GSYİH) with dominant services sector (76.5% GSYİH, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership şeffaflık requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership şeffaflık. Ne zaman financial secrecy is eliminated, the örtüşen çıkar between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity zayıflar.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's AB üyesiship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Uygulamak enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
Benimsemek full beneficial ownership şeffaflık ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: Güçlendirmek beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: Uygulatmak enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce şeffaflık standards
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Βουλευτής in the Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) talep etmek için full implementation of EU AML directives
Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced şeffaflık requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
Şunu talep edin: EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an AB üyesi with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial ekonomi ($347B GSYİH) with strong manufacturing (19.9% GSYİH, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). AB üyesi. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Hizmetler 60.2% GSYİH.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite madencilik is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the örtüşen çıkar between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Ne zaman plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the örtüşen çıkar breaks.
Mandatory santral başına emisyonlar and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
Kırılamaz. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report emisyonlar violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in madencilik communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech ihbarcı protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)
ČEZ (majority devlete ait): Accelerate coal phase-out timeline and yenilenebilir enerji investment
Sev.en Energy: Geliştirmek mine closure and site remediation plans
Yatırım yapmak nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Reform yapmak electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Güçlendirmek air quality enforcement in madencilik regions
Czech Coal Commission: Uygulamak recommendations on coal phase-out by 2033
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: Poslanec or Senátor in the Parlament talep etmek için a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition measures
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform
Şunu talep edin: Territorial Just Transition Plans with concrete retraining programs
i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for çevresel law, conducting inspections and imposing yaptırımlar. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees çevresel policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% devlete ait), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite madencilik and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent enerji düzenleyicisi. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic ekonomi ($424.5B GSYİH) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Hizmetler 63.5% GSYİH. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Reform yapmak agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Ne zaman the örtüşen çıkar between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates sistem bağımlılığı can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)
Document animal welfare violations — report to Fødevarestyrelsen
Use Danish ihbarcı law (Lov om beskyttelse af ihbarcıe, 2021)
Danish Crown, Arla: Yatırım yapmak plant-based protein alternatives
Uygulamak nitrogen-neutral farming targets across supply chains
Benimsemek Scope 3 agricultural emisyonlar reporting
Miljøministeriet: Tighten nitrogen quotas for marine ecosystem recovery
Landbrugsstyrelsen: Reform yapmak CAP eco-scheme payments to reward nitrogen-negative farming
Uygulamak the 2021 agreement on green transition of Danish agriculture (55-65% emisyonlar reduction by 2030)
Temsilcinizle iletişime geçin: member of the Folketing talep etmek için enforcement of agricultural nitrogen reduction targets
Support EU-wide mandatory nitrogen balance disclosure
Advocate for carbon border adjustment on imported animal feed from ormansızlaşma regions
Şunu talep edin: Baltic Sea Action Plan implementation through HELCOM
i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for çevresel regulation including agricultural emisyonlar and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — çevresel policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% emisyonlar reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest ekonomi ($124.3B GSYİH) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and madencilik (2.04% GSYİH — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Hizmetler 59.8% GSYİH. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Ne zaman maden başına water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: Mandate çevresel bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Ne zaman madencilik companies pre-fund cleanup, örtüşen çıkar extraction without çevresel accountability zayıflar.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from madencilik revenue.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Open-pit madencilik disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce sistem bağımlılığı.

i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the madencilik directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees madencilik policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs madencilik concessions and çevresel obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential demokrasi.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B GSYİH) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Tarım 14% GSYİH (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Ne zaman production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, örtüşen çıkar continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Ne zaman solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, örtüşen çıkar gas-fired power generation breaks.

Uygulamak saha başına emisyonlar disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes çevresel damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines

ARH: Uygulatmak flaring reduction regulations

i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive ihbarcı protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent ekonomi ($124.7B GSYİH) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Tarım 9.5% GSYİH (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the örtüşen çıkar. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Uygulamak the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Ne zaman democratic decisions override extraction interests, the örtüşen çıkar breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block çevresel impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's çevresel data (Amazon ormansızlaşma, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: MAATE (Ministerio del Ambiente, Agua y Transición Ecológica) — Ecuador's environment ministry. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman. Ecuador's 2008 Constitution (Articles 71-74) grants legally enforceable rights to nature (Pachamama), a global first.

EP PetroEcuador — the state oil company. PetroAmazonas EP — manages upstream operations in the Amazon basin. The Lago Agrio case (Chevron/Texaco) — one of the world's largest çevresel lawsuits over oil contamination in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) — the energy and natural resources regulatory agency.

Asamblea Nacional del Ecuador — unicameral parliament (137 members). Contact through asambleanacional.gob.ec. Ecuador's rights of nature framework (2008 Constitution) and the 2023 Yasuní referendum are globally significant legal precedents for çevresel governance.

EE.s: Advanced digital ekonomi ($43.1B GSYİH) with unique oil shale dependence (0.96% oil rents from kukersite extraction). Services-dominant (65.7% GSYİH). EU and NATO member. World leader in e-governance. Oil shale power generation is Estonia's largest source of CO₂ emisyonlar.

sectors.w: Estonia's oil shale sector is concentrated in Ida-Viru county (Eesti Energia/Enefit). EU ETS carbon costs make oil shale electricity the most expensive fossil option. Ne zaman carbon pricing fully applies, the örtüşen çıkar between shale operators and electricity consumers breaks.
bp: Full EU ETS alignment without compensation. Oil shale electricity already costs more than wind in the Baltic. Ne zaman subsidies and free allowances are eliminated, örtüşen çıkar oil shale generation breaks — renewables are cheaper.
Mandatory santral başına lifecycle emisyonlar and health-impact disclosure for oil shale processing. Estonia's detailed çevresel monitoring makes W computable.
Kırılamaz. Oil shale processing releases CO₂, particulate matter, and phenolic wastewater by physical and chemical process.

Document health impacts in Ida-Viru communities

i: Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board) — Estonia's çevresel enforcement and permitting authority. Keskkonnaministeerium (Ministry of the Environment) — oversees çevresel policy. Estonia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Eesti Energia (Enefit) — the devlete ait energy company and dominant oil shale operator. Enefit operates the Narva power plants (the largest oil shale power plants in the world) and oil shale processing facilities.

Konkurentsiamet (Competition Authority) — also serves as Estonia's enerji düzenleyicisi. Ida-Viru county hosts ~90% of Estonia's oil shale operations and faces significant economic transition challenges.

Riigikogu — Estonia's unicameral parliament (101 members). Contact through riigikogu.ee. Estonia's Climate Ambition target includes significant emisyonlar reduction from the energy sector. EU Just Transition Fund — Estonia receives JTF funding for Ida-Viru transition.

EG.s: Largest Arab ekonomi by population ($389.1B GSYİH). Oil and gas rents at 5.04%. Major gas producer (Zohr field, Mediterranean). Tarım 13.7% GSYİH. High military expenditure. Hosted COP27. Suez Canal provides strategic global trade leverage.

sectors.w: Egypt's Zohr gas field (discovered 2015) transformed Egypt from gas importer to exporter. COP27 presidency created domestic pressure for emisyonlar şeffaflık. Per-field emisyonlar disclosure makes W computable.
bp: Redirect gas export revenue to solar energy development. Egypt's solar irradiance is among the world's highest. Ne zaman solar electricity undercuts gas-fired generation domestically, örtüşen çıkar gas-for-domestic-power zayıflar.

Uygulamak saha başına emisyonlar disclosure for Zohr and other major fields. Egypt's COP27 commitments create institutional pressure — making lifecycle emisyonlar visible per unit of production makes W computable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.

i: EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency) — Egypt's çevresel regulatory body. EGPC (Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil and gas company. EGAS (Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company) — manages natural gas operations. Note: Egypt has severe restrictions on sivil toplum and press freedom. Whistleblower protections are minimal.
Eni — operates Zohr, the largest gas field in the Mediterranean. BP — major operator in the West Nile Delta. EGPC and EGAS manage state interests in all petroleum operations.

Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources — oversees hydrocarbons policy. NREA (New and Renewable Energy Authority) — responsible for yenilenebilir enerji development. Egypt's Integrated Sustainable Energy Strategy targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035.

Note: Egypt under President el-Sisi is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The House of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 596 members) does not function as an independent democratic institution. International channels: Arab League, African Union (Egypt hosted AU in 2019), Union for the Mediterranean, COP27 legacy initiatives.

ET.s: Africa's second-most-populous country ($149.7B GSYİH). Agriculture dominates (34.8% GSYİH, primarily coffee, teff, livestock). Rapid industrialization drive (industrial parks). Major hydropower (GERD — Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam). Post-civil war recovery.

sectors.w: Ethiopia's agricultural expansion is the primary driver of ormansızlaşma (fr=5.6% GSYİH equivalent in forest rents). Ne zaman per-farm çevresel data (tree cover, soil carbon) is linked to agricultural output, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: Scale Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Program (PSNP) to include çevresel conditionality. Ne zaman agricultural support payments require soil conservation and tree planting, örtüşen çıkar clearing forest for short-term cultivation zayıflar.

Satellite-based monitoring of forest cover and toprak bozulması published at woreda level. Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative provides political commitment — mandatory monitoring data makes W computable.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Subsistence agriculture on degraded land inherently affects soil and water systems. However, Ethiopia's traditional conservation agriculture (terracing, agroforestry) can reduce sistem bağımlılığı.

i: Regional çevresel protection authorities — Ethiopia's federal system devolves çevresel management to regional states. EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission) — the national human rights institution. Geliştirmekment Agents (DAs) — government agricultural extension workers deployed at kebele (ward) level throughout rural Ethiopia.

ECX (Ethiopian Commodity Exchange) — the commodities trading platform for coffee, sesame, and other agricultural products. Ethiopia is Africa's largest coffee producer and coffee is the country's largest export. Industrial parks development corporation — manages Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy and the PSNP. EFCCC (Environment, Forest and Climate Change Commission) — Ethiopia's çevre otoritesi. Green Legacy Initiative — the government's flagship reforestation program targeting 20 billion seedlings.

Note: Ethiopia is a federal parlamenter republic but democratic governance has been constrained by civil conflict (Tigray war 2020-2022). The House of Peoples' Representatives (547 members) is the lower house. International channels: African Union (HQ in Addis Ababa), Nile Basin Initiative, IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Geliştirmekment), AFR100 (Africa's contribution to the Bonn Challenge on forest restoration).

FI.s: Advanced Nordic ekonomi ($298.7B GSYİH) with strong forestry sector. Hizmetler 62.2% GSYİH, manufacturing at 14.2%. High healthcare expenditure (10.47% GSYİH). Major pulp and paper producers (UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group). AB üyesi with robust çevresel regulation.

sectors.w: Finland's forestry-driven monoculture (pine/spruce plantations replacing natural forests) reduces biodiversity and degrades peatland carbon stocks. Ne zaman per-harvest biodiversity and carbon data is published alongside timber revenue, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: Reform yapmak EU CAP and national forestry subsidies to reward biodiversity and carbon storage rather than timber volume. Ne zaman subsidies incentivize continuous-cover forestry over clear-cutting, örtüşen çıkar plantation monoculture zayıflar.

Mandatory per-harvest çevresel data (biodiversity indicators, soil carbon, water quality). Finland's Metsähallitus (state forest enterprise) already collects extensive data — making it publicly accessible makes W computable.

Kısmen kırılabilir. Timber plantation inherently reduces biodiversity compared to natural forest. However, continuous-cover forestry and extended rotation lengths can significantly reduce sistem bağımlılığı.

i: ELY-keskus — regional authorities responsible for çevresel monitoring and enforcement. SYKE (Suomen ympäristökeskus / Finnish Environment Institute) — the national çevresel research and monitoring institute. Ilmoittajansuojelulaki (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) — Finnish transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

UPM-Kymmene — one of Finland's largest forestry companies. Stora Enso — Finnish-Swedish forestry and paper company. Metsä Group — cooperative of 100,000+ Finnish forest owners. Metsähallitus — state enterprise managing one-third of Finland's land area.

MMM (Maa- ja metsätalousministeriö) — the ministry responsible for agriculture and forestry policy. National Forest Strategy 2025 — Finland's strategic framework for forestry sector development. EU Nature Restoration Law — requires restoration of degraded ecosystems including forests.

Eduskunta (Parliament of Finland) — unicameral legislature with 200 members. Contact through eduskunta.fi. The Maa- ja metsätalousvaliokunta (Agriculture and Forestry Committee) handles forestry legislation. Finland's climate law targets carbon neutrality by 2035.

GA.s: Oil-dependent Central African ekonomi ($20.9B GSYİH) with 15.83% combined oil and gas rents. 88% forest cover — one of the world's most forested countries. Low population (2.4M). Military coup August 2023 ended the Bongo family's 55-year rule.

sectors.w: Gabon's oil production is in terminal decline from mature fields (peaked at ~370,000 bpd in 1997, now ~200,000). As production falls, the örtüşen çıkar between operators and government revenue zayıflar. Gabon's forests offer alternative revenue through carbon credits.
bp: Monetize Gabon's forest carbon stocks through REDD+ and voluntary carbon markets. Gabon received $17M from Norway for forest protection in 2021 — scaling this replaces declining oil revenue and breaks örtüşen çıkar extraction.
Uygulamak saha başına emisyonlar disclosure for all operators (TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala Energy). Making çevresel costs visible makes W computable.
Kırılamaz by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

i: ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux) — manages Gabon's 13 national parks (covering 11% of territory). GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company. EITI Gabon — member since 2004. Note: Gabon is under military transition government since August 2023 coup.
TotalEnergies — the largest oil producer in Gabon. Perenco, Assala Energy — other major operators. GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company.
Ministry of Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas policy. DGEPN (Direction Générale de l'Environnement et de la Protection de la Nature) — çevresel directorate.

Note: Gabon is under military transition government (Comité pour la Transition et la Restauration des Institutions, CTRI) since August 2023. Democratic institutions are suspended. International channels: CEMAC, CAFI, African Union, OPEC membership. Gabon's 2021 deal with Norway's sovereign wealth fund for forest protection is a model for forest-state climate finance.

GE.s: Small South Caucasus ekonomi ($34.2B GSYİH) with madencilik sector (1.28% GSYİH, primarily manganese and copper). Services-dominant (62% GSYİH). AB adayı country (2023). Significant hydropower potential. Political tensions between pro-EU and pro-Russia orientations.

sectors.w: Georgia's manganese madencilik (Chiatura) and copper madencilik generate significant water pollution. EU accession çevresel alignment will require improved monitoring. Ne zaman water quality data is linked to madencilik output, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir.
bp: EU accession çevresel alignment. Ne zaman Georgia must meet EU çevresel standards for madencilik, örtüşen çıkar low-cost extraction without çevresel controls zayıflar.
Mandatory maden başına water quality and emisyonlar disclosure. Georgia's National Environmental Agency collects some data — extending to public disclosure makes W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Mining disturbs landscapes, but improved tailings management can reduce sistem bağımlılığı.

i: NEA (National Environmental Agency) — responsible for çevresel monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Agriculture — oversees çevresel and agricultural policy. Georgia's Law on Freedom of Speech and Expression provides some protections for reporting.

Georgian Manganese — subsidiary of ERG (Eurasian Resources Group), operating the Chiatura manganese mines. RMG Copper — operates copper mines. Georgia's madencilik sector is primarily controlled by international investors.
National Agency of Mines — responsible for madencilik licensing and oversight. Georgia's EU Association Agreement (2014) includes provisions for çevresel alignment.

Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) — unicameral legislature with 150 members. Contact through parliament.ge. Georgia is an AB adayı country (2023) and aspirant to full AB üyesiship. EU Association Agreement — includes Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Area (DCFTA) with çevresel provisions.

GH.s: West African ekonomi ($82.3B GSYİH) with significant oil (4.06% rents), gold madencilik (5.17% mineral rents), and cocoa agriculture (20.9% ag share). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Debt crisis (2022 IMF bailout). Illegal artisanal gold madencilik (galamsey) is a major çevresel crisis.

sectors.w: Ghana's galamsey (illegal artisanal madencilik) crisis has contaminated water bodies across cocoa-growing regions. Ne zaman maden başına water quality data is published and linked to gold production, W hesaplanabilir hale gelir. The galamsey problem is so severe that it threatens Ghana's water supply.
bp: Formalize artisanal madencilik with çevresel standards and alternative livelihood programs. Ne zaman legal small-scale madencilik provides comparable income with less çevresel damage, örtüşen çıkar illegal extraction zayıflar.
Satellite-based water quality monitoring linked to madencilik areas. Ghana's Water Resources Commission already monitors water quality — extending to real-time public data makes W computable.
Kısmen kırılabilir. Mining disturbs landscapes. However, formalized artisanal madencilik with mercury-free processing (e.g., gravity separation) can significantly reduce sistem bağımlılığı.

Uygulamak community development agreements per Ghana's madencilik law
Benimsemek mercury-free processing technology transfer programs
Minerals Commission: Accelerate small-scale madencilik formalization with çevresel standards
EPA (Environmental Protection Agency): Güçlendirmek enforcement against water pollution from madencilik
Forestry Commission: Protect forest reserves from galamsey encroachment
